topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The bank statement, stark white under the harsh kitchen light, was an insult. My hands trembled as I traced the numbers—a sum that represented months of carefully concealed planning, a life I hadn't been privy to. I felt a hot, tight knot constricting my chest, radiating outward into my jaw. I snatched the paper, the sound of tearing the edge echoing unnervingly loud in the silence. "How could you?" I hissed, the words tasting like ash. Every muscle in my body was coiled tight, ready to spring. I looked at Mark, who was humming softly while reading the newspaper, and the sight of his placid expression only intensified the churning inside me. I crumpled the paper into a tight ball and threw it onto the counter, the sound sharp and final.

[story 2]

I found the hidden folder tucked behind the spare linens in the guest room. The contents were irrefutable: a ledger detailing transfers, a series of accounts labeled with cryptic initials. A wave of intense agitation washed over me, making my vision narrow. I didn't bother reading every line; the sheer audacity of the deception was enough to make my breath catch. My knuckles were white where I gripped the edge of the desk. "This is beyond betrayal," I managed to say, my voice dangerously low and steady, though the tremor in my hands betrayed the turmoil beneath the surface. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small room like a caged animal, the floorboards groaning under my sudden, restless movements.

[story 3]

It was a quiet Tuesday afternoon, the kind of calm that made the discovery all the more jarring. The notification pop-up on the shared online banking portal had been too obvious, too glaring. When I opened the account, the balance stared back at me—a significant amount, entirely undocumented. A deep, visceral reaction seized me; my stomach clenched, and I had to press my palms hard against my thighs to keep from shaking. I stared at the screen, my gaze burning holes through the digital numbers. I walked over to Mark, who was attempting to assemble some flat-pack furniture, and the sudden intrusion of my intense stillness made him look up, sensing the seismic shift in the atmosphere. "Explain this," I demanded, my tone clipped, every syllable weighted with suppressed intensity.
